---
name: competitive-intel
description: Systematic competitor tracking that feeds positioning, battlecards, and roadmap decisions. Use when analyzing competitors, building sales battlecards, tracking market moves, or positioning against alternatives.
type: Playbook
---

# Competitive Intelligence

Systematic competitor tracking. Not obsession — intelligence that drives real decisions.

## 5-Layer Intelligence System

### Layer 1: Competitor Identification

**Direct:** Same ICP, same problem, comparable solution, similar price.
**Indirect:** Same budget, different solution (including "do nothing" and "build in-house").
**Future:** Well-funded startups in adjacent space; large incumbents with roadmap overlap.

**2x2 Threat Matrix:**

| | Same ICP | Different ICP |
|---|---|---|
| **Same problem** | Direct threat | Adjacent (watch) |
| **Different problem** | Displacement risk | Ignore for now |

### Layer 2: Tracking Dimensions (8 per competitor)

| Dimension | Sources | Cadence |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Product moves | Changelog, G2/Capterra, Twitter/LinkedIn | Monthly |
| Pricing changes | Pricing page, sales calls, customer feedback | Triggered |
| Funding | Crunchbase, TechCrunch, LinkedIn | Triggered |
| Hiring signals | LinkedIn job postings | Monthly |
| Partnerships | Press releases, co-marketing | Triggered |
| Customer wins | Case studies, review sites | Monthly |
| Customer losses | Win/loss interviews, churned accounts | Ongoing |
| Messaging shifts | Homepage, ad libraries | Quarterly |

### Layer 3: Analysis Frameworks

**SWOT per competitor:** Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats.

**Competitive Positioning Map (2 axes):** Choose axes that matter for buyers (Price vs Feature Depth, Enterprise vs SMB, Easy vs Configurable). Pick axes that show YOUR differentiation.

**Feature Gap Analysis:**

| Feature | You | Competitor A | Competitor B | Gap status |
|---------|-----|-------------|-------------|------------|
| [Feature] | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | Your advantage |
| [Feature] | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | Gap — roadmap? |

### Layer 4: Output Formats

- **For Sales (CRO):** Battlecards — one page per competitor, pre-call prep
- **For Marketing (CMO):** Positioning update — message shifts, new differentiators
- **For Product (CPO):** Feature gap summary — what customers ask for, what competitors ship
- **For CEO/Board:** Monthly competitive summary — 1 page: who moved, what it means, recommended responses

### Layer 5: Intelligence Cadence

**Monthly:** Review tier-1 competitors, update battlecards, publish 1-page summary.
**Triggered:** Funding round → assess within 48h. Major feature launch → response within 1 week. Key customer loss → win/loss interview within 2 weeks.
**Quarterly:** Full landscape review, update positioning map, refresh ICP threat assessment.

## Win/Loss Analysis

Highest-signal competitive data. Most companies do it too rarely.

**When to interview:** Every lost deal >$50K ACV, every churn >6 months tenure, every competitive win.
**Who conducts it:** NOT the AE who worked the deal. Customer success, product team, or external researcher.

**Question structure:**
1. "Walk me through your evaluation process"
2. "Who else were you considering?"
3. "What were the top 3 criteria?"
4. "Where did [our product] fall short?"
5. "What was the deciding factor?"
6. "What would have changed your decision?"

## The Balance: Intelligence Without Obsession

**Over-tracking signs:** Roadmap driven by "they just shipped X", team morale drops on competitor fundraise, shipping features you don't believe in.

**Under-tracking signs:** AEs get blindsided, prospects know more than your team, missed major product launch, positioning unchanged in 12+ months.

**Right posture:** Know competitors well enough to win. Don't let them set your agenda. Roadmap led by customer problems, informed by competitive gaps.

## Distribution

| Audience | Format | Cadence | Owner |
|----------|--------|---------|-------|
| AEs + SDRs | Updated battlecards in CRM | Monthly + triggered | CRO |
| Product | Feature gap analysis | Quarterly | CPO |
| Marketing | Positioning brief | Quarterly | CMO |
| Leadership | 1-page competitive summary | Monthly | CEO/COO |
| Board | Competitive landscape slide | Quarterly | CEO |

**One source of truth:** All competitive intel lives in one place (Notion, Confluence, Salesforce). Avoid Slack-only distribution.

## Red Flags

| Signal | What it means |
|--------|---------------|
| Competitor win rate >50% in core segment | Fundamental positioning problem |
| Same objection from 5+ deals | Real feature gap |
| Competitor hired 10 engineers in your domain | Major product investment incoming |
| Competitor raised >$20M targeting your ICP | 12-month runway to compete hard |
| Prospects evaluate you to justify competitor decision | You're the "check box" |
